text: Entire frame solid black; no visible lecture content, text, diagrams, axes, symbols, or objects.

Speaker centered in frame, face obscured by a large vertical blur rectangle. Light blue collared shirt.

Background: beige walls, white ceiling. Left wall: four framed pictures arranged in a 2×2 grouping above a dark sofa/backrest—top left larger frame with pale abstract content and a small circular element near lower area; top right narrow frame with two dark vertical shapes; bottom left small pale frame; bottom right small pale frame. Right wall: large dark-framed artwork with patchwork-style pattern including red cross-like bands, black-and-white checkerboard sections, and a smaller central framed square. Lower right: red cushion or folded fabric on seating.

Warm ceiling light visible near top center. Small video label at bottom left: “Rich HWY”.

Title: “Pathology in Parkinson’s disease”

Top text: “Loss of dopamine-producing cells in substantia nigra”

Left figure: grayscale coronal brain section; red arrow points to a dark region in the lower midbrain area.

Right figure: two gross brain slices side by side with labels at top:
- “PD Brain”
- “Normal Brain”
Red arrows point to dark pigmented regions in both slices.

Centered below figures: “Substantia nigra”

Lower left heading: “Risk factors”
Bullet-style list:
- “genetics (low)”
- “environment (e.g., pesticides)”
- “infection with flu”
- “traumatic brain injury”

Lower right figure: small forest plot.
Legend text:
- “All influenza cases”
- “EIV infected, y”
Rows:
- “<5”
- “>5-10”
- “>10”
Bottom axis label: “Odds ratio (95% CI)”
Horizontal confidence-interval lines with central markers; x-axis ticks labeled “0”, “2”, “4”.

Title: “Pathology in Parkinson’s disease”

Main statement under title: “Loss of dopamine-producing cells in substantia nigra”

Left image: grayscale coronal brain section. Red arrow points to a dark region in the midbrain.  
Right image: two photographed brain slices side by side with faint labels at top: “PD Brain” over the left slice, “Normal Brain” over the right slice. Red arrows point to corresponding dark band/region in each slice.

Centered label beneath the two images: “Substantia nigra”

Lower left text block, heading: “Risk factors”
- “genetics (low)”
- “environment (e.g., pesticides)”
- “infection with flu”
- “traumatic brain injury”

- “Ever infected, y”
- “≤5”
- “>5-10”
- “>10”

Horizontal axis label: “Odds ratio (95% CI)” with tick marks including 0, 2, 4. Multiple points with horizontal confidence intervals plotted across rows.

Title: **Parkinson’s Disease**

Legend at upper left:
- green square: **Excitatory connection**
- red square: **Inhibitory connection**

Two basal ganglia pathway diagrams side by side.

Left diagram:
- Top box: **Cortex**
- Below: **Striatum**
- Center left label: **Hyperdirect**
- Mid left nucleus: **GPe**
- Mid lower center nucleus: **STN**
- Lower center nucleus: **GPi/SNr**
- Lower small box: **SNc**
- Right mid box: **Thalamus**
- Bottom left text: **Brainstem spinal cord**
- Right side label near red pathway: **Direct**
- Left-center label near red/green pathway: **Indirect**
- Green excitatory arrows:
  - long arrow from **Cortex** down left side to **Brainstem spinal cord**
  - arrow from **Cortex** down to **STN** (hyperdirect)
  - arrow from **STN** to **GPi/SNr**
  - arrow from **Thalamus** up to **Cortex**
  - arrow from **SNc** up to **Striatum**
- Red inhibitory arrows:
  - from **Striatum** to **GPi/SNr** (direct pathway)
  - from **Striatum** to **GPe** (indirect pathway)
  - from **GPe** to **STN**
  - from **GPi/SNr** to **Thalamus**

Right diagram:
- Top box: **Cortex**
- Below: **Striatum**
- Mid left nucleus: **GPe**
- Mid lower center nucleus: **STN**
- Lower center nucleus: **GPi**
- Small box between striatum and GPi: **SNc**
- Right mid box: **Thalamus**
- Bottom left text: **Brainstem spinal cord**
- Right side label: **Direct**
- Left-center label: **Indirect**
- Green arrows:
